instructions:
Upgrade `.docs-ops/extractors/typescript-extractor.py` from v1.2 to v1.3 to capture enum members (cases) inside enums declared in the `declare global { namespace Amity { ... } }` blocks.

CONTEXT: v1.2 captures enum DECLARATIONS in the Amity namespace (e.g. `Amity.SessionStates`, `Amity.AdPlacement`, `Amity.StoryItemType`) but does NOT capture the individual enum cases inside them. The current drift report shows refs like `Amity.SessionStates.ESTABLISHED`, `Amity.AdPlacement.FEED`, `Amity.StoryItemType.Hyperlink` getting flagged as unknown because the validator can't see the case names.

WHAT TO ADD: when the extract_amity_namespace() function finds an enum declaration inside the Amity namespace block, it should also walk into the enum body and capture each case as a synthetic member.

SCHEMA SHAPE — extend the existing Amity namespace member entries:

  Existing v1.2 enum entry:
    { "name": "SessionStates", "kind": "enum", "file": "...", "line": N }

  v1.3 should ALSO surface each case as a separate dotted member, like:
    { "name": "SessionStates.ESTABLISHED", "kind": "enum_case", "file": "...", "line": N, "parent_enum": "SessionStates" }

  OR (alternative cleaner schema): make the enum entry hold a `cases: [...]` sub-array, AND the lookup_path logic in the validator already walks down to multi-level paths so a parallel synthetic entry isn't strictly necessary IF the validator is updated.

PICK ONE approach — either is fine. Prefer the dotted-member approach if it requires no validator changes; prefer the sub-array approach if it's structurally cleaner. Document the choice in `notes`.

IF you choose the dotted-member approach: just add new entries to `members[]` of the Amity namespace with dotted names. The validator's `lookup_path` already supports dotted refs; it would naturally find `Amity.SessionStates.ESTABLISHED` if such a member exists. Verify this works.

IF you choose the sub-array approach: update the validator's `lookup_path` to recognize enum cases — when walking a path and the current node is an enum member with a `cases` array, the next segment may be in that array.

IMPLEMENTATION:

1. In the existing AMITY_NS_DECL_PATTERNS or similar, when the matched declaration kind is `enum` or `const enum`, ALSO parse the body braces to find case names.
2. Use brace-depth tracking (similar to how the namespace block itself is delimited). Inside the enum body, match patterns:
   - `<Name>,` — bare enum case (defaulted)
   - `<Name> = <value>,` — enum case with explicit value
   - `<Name>,` at end of body (no trailing comma also valid)
3. Case names are typically `UPPER_SNAKE` or `PascalCase` — don't filter on naming; just capture `[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*` followed by `=` or `,` or `}` (after stripping comments and whitespace).
4. Skip lines that are clearly NOT case declarations (comments starting with `//` or `/*`, blank lines, multi-line string/expression values that span lines).
5. Bump extractor_version to `0.1.3`.
6. Add stat `amity_enum_cases_total`.

After the change, RE-RUN the extractor + validator and verify:
  - `Amity.SessionStates.ESTABLISHED`, `Amity.AdPlacement.FEED`, `Amity.StoryItemType.Hyperlink` (and similar) no longer appear in the drift report.
  - The total issue count drops by the number of previously-flagged enum-case refs (expect ~6-10 fewer).
  - No NEW false negatives — refs that should still be flagged still are.

Report in `notes`: which schema approach you picked and why, total enum cases captured across the Amity namespace, before/after drift issue counts, and any edge cases (string-valued enums, computed values, conditional cases via TS spread).

validators.name: amity_enum_cases_resolved
validators.command: python3 .docs-ops/extractors/typescript-extractor.py > /dev/null && python3 .docs-ops/validators/ts-accuracy-validator.py > /dev/null && python3 -c "import json; r=json.load(open('.docs-ops/evals/ts-accuracy-drift.json')); s=json.load(open('.docs-ops/sdk-surface/typescript.json')); known_enum_cases={'Amity.SessionStates.ESTABLISHED','Amity.AdPlacement.FEED','Amity.StoryItemType.Hyperlink','Amity.InvitationTargetType.Room'}; still=set(); [still.add(i['ref']) for f,iss in r['issues_by_file'].items() for i in iss if i.get('ref') in known_enum_cases]; assert s['extractor_version']=='0.1.3', f'version not bumped: {s[\"extractor_version\"]}'; print('FAIL: still flagging '+str(still)) if still else print('OK'); import sys; sys.exit(1 if still else 0)"
